title: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Campaign Monitor: pricing, features, and performance to find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
h1: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor: Which Email Marketing Tool Wins?
intro: Buttondown and Campaign Monitor serve different audiences in the email marketing space. Buttondown targets developers and newsletter creators with a freemium model and markdown support, while Campaign Monitor focuses on agencies managing multiple brands with advanced design and segmentation tools. This comparison helps you decide which platform aligns with your marketing needs and budget.

comparison_table.tool_b: Journey designer, smart segments, transactional email

choose_a_if: You're a developer or technical writer who prefers writing in markdown
You want to start free and scale affordably as your audience grows
You need API access and want to build custom integrations
choose_b_if: You're managing multiple brands or client accounts within one platform
You need advanced design tools and visual email builders
You require transactional email capabilities and sophisticated automation journeys

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your use case. Buttondown excels for individual writers and developers with its free tier and markdown support, earning higher ratings (4.8 vs 4.1 on G2). Campaign Monitor is more powerful for agencies managing multiple brands with its journey designer and multi-brand management features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper. It offers a free tier for up to 100 subscribers, while Campaign Monitor has no free option and starts at $11/month. Buttondown's paid tier begins at just $9/month, making it more accessible for bootstrapped creators and small publishers.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality. Buttondown explicitly offers import/export capabilities, making it relatively straightforward to migrate your subscriber lists. However, you'll lose advanced features like journey design if switching from Campaign Monitor, so evaluate whether Buttondown's simpler feature set meets your needs.
